title: The datey specification
text: datey system provides standard mapping dates onto annual grid. matters contexts primary unit years input data uses dates (.e. days)1. goal datey provide performant date duration-related arithmetic intervals input dates. following non goals handled outside datey system: Real-world date arithmetic2, including calculating dates output. Parsing formatting dates.

title: The datey specification
text: double means IEEE 754 binary64, .e. 64-bit binary floating-point. integer means 32 bit two’s complement signed integer. Dates written using YYYY‑MM‑DD notation, YYYY four-digit year, MM two-digit month year, 01 12, DD two-digit day month, 01 31. Banker’s rounding3 means round double nearest integer unless fractional part ±0.5, case round nearest even integer. algorithms , also entails conversion integer, safe cases algorithms guarantee value convert lies within range integer.

title: The datey specification
text: datey system assumes following: calendar years duration. Within calendar year, days duration. Time granular, smallest indivisible unit click, 1 / 534 360 year. variation arising , instance, time zones, daylight saving time leap seconds scope – allowance must done outside datey system.

title: The datey specification
text: datey system defines two types: datey represents date. durationy represents difference two dates. types represented using clicks stored integer.

title: The datey specification
text: Dates mapped dateys number clicks since start notional year 0000 proleptic Gregorian calendar, .e. Gregorian calendar extended backwards introduction 1582. datey otherwise represent date calendar year 1000 calendar year 2999 treated invalid4 mapping dates datey.

title: The datey specification
text: durationy represents duration using clicks. durationy otherwise represent duration magnitude 2000 years treated invalid mapping durations durationy.

what: Mapping years to a datey or durationy
title: The datey specification
text: datey can defined direct duration years start notional year 0000 durationy can defined direct duration years follows: duration years represented numerical type double must first converted double. entails loss precision error. result invalid following apply: duration years number (NaN). datey, duration years less 1000 greater 3000. durationy, absolute value duration years greater 2000. Otherwise resulting number clicks duration years multiplied 534 360 rounded integer using banker’s rounding. Worked examples:

what: Mapping a date to a datey
title: The datey specification
text: mapping date specified calendar year, month day datey point within day also needs specified. typical use, expected start, middle end day specified, mapping including day-fraction specified full generality. mapping function integer year, month day, double day_fraction follows: Valid inputs year: integer 1000 2999 inclusive month: integer 1 12 inclusive day: integer 1 number days month specified year month day_fraction: double 0 1 inclusive addition, special case 0999-12-31 day_fraction precisely equal 1 also treated valid. year, month, day day_fraction invalid result invalid. Define clicks_per_day 1460 year leap year 1464 otherwise. Define integer days_to_start_of_month number days start year start month (allowing whether year leap year). Define integer day_count days_to_start_of_month + day − 1. Define integer fraction_clicks day_fraction × clicks_per_day rounded integer using banker’s rounding. resulting number clicks defined integer calculation year × 534 360 + day_count × clicks_per_day + fraction_clicks avoidance doubt, special case 2999-12-31 day_fraction rounds 1 returned . convenience, datey implementation also provide mapping datey start, middle end date using follows. start_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 0.0) mid_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 0.5) end_day(year, month, day) := datey_from_YMDF(year, month, day, 1.0) Worked examples:

what: Mapping a datey to a date and day-fraction
title: The datey specification
text: datey mapped clicks year, month, day day_fraction follows: clicks < 1000 × 534 360 clicks ≥ 3000 × 534 360 datey invalid therefore result also invalid. year clicks div 534 360, div means integer division rounded nearest integer. Define clicks_remaining clicks − year × 534 360. Define clicks_per_day 1460 year leap year 1464 otherwise. Define day_in_year clicks_remaining div clicks_per_day, div means integer division rounded nearest integer. Use day_in_year determine month day. Define day_fraction_clicks clicks_remaining − day_in_year × clicks_per_day. day_fraction day_fraction_clicks / clicks_per_day using double division.

title: The datey specification
text: following arithmetic operations defined applying specified operators underlying argument clicks using 32-bit two’s complement arithmetic. unary plus (+) minus (negation −) operators defined durationy: + operator returns argument unchanged. − operator changes sign clicks. required expected operations checked integer overflow – can reasonably supposed safe within domain operation (.e. date inputs already checked validity) datey exists facilitate performant calculations.

what: Mixed datey / durationy and numeric operations
title: The datey specification
text: convenience, binary operations also defined one operands datey duration numeric. first step convert datey duration operand years follows: datey durationy invalid result number (NaN). Otherwise result clicks converted double divided 534 360. required numeric operand converted double. entails loss precision error. following standard operations legal

title: The datey specification
text: datey system provides simple, standardised text input output format. complicated scenarios, dates parsed formatted outside datey system.

title: The datey specification
text: valid datey represented text format YYYY‑MM‑DD.FFF YYYY four-digit year, MM two-digit month year (starting “01” January) DD two-digit day month (first day month “01”), .FFF optional day fraction (‘.’ present least 1 digit must also provided). text outputs: fraction contains 3 decimal places (sufficient distinguish possible day fractions datey precision). option whether zero day fraction stated explicitly (e.g. ‘2000-01-01.0’). text inputs: Blank fractions treated zero. fractional part constrained 3 decimal places. Arbitrarily long inputs e.g. 100 UTF-8 bytes rejected.

title: The datey specification
text: Unless durationy serialised, recommended durations parsed formatted using conventional means applied duration measured years. valid durationy represented text format SY.FFFFFF UUU S plus minus sign, .e. one ‘+’ (U+002B), true minus ‘−’ (U+2212) ASCII hyphen-minus ‘-’ (U+002D). Y number whole years .FFFFFF optional fractional part year. UUU unit name one year. UUU non-blank preceded space. UUU longer 20 UTF-8 bytes contain control characters. text outputs: optional whether ‘+’ (U+002B) plus sign included positive durations optional. (Default ‘+’). optional whether use true minus sign ‘−’ (U+002B) ASCII ‘-’ (U+002D) hyphen-minus character. (Default true minus sign.) fraction contains 6 decimal places (sufficient distinguish possible duration fractions datey precision). unit user-specifiable. (Default ‘yrs’.) text inputs: plus minus sign included parsed (including zero durations). fractional part constrained 6 decimal places. unit user-specifiable. non-blank unit text must present preceded space. (default ’ yrs’.) Arbitrarily long inputs e.g. 100 UTF-8 bytes rejected.

text: Gordon T (2026). datey: Annualised Dates Durations. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/logmu-org/r-datey.
code: @Manual{,   title = {datey: Annualised Dates and Durations},   author = {Tim Gordon},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/logmu-org/r-datey}, }

title: Annualised Dates and Durations
text: datey package provides standard mapping dates onto annual grid. matters contexts primary unit years definitions need precise. Classic examples mortality experience analysis valuation life assurance annuities. Mortality rates defined per annum experience valuation data usually defined using dates (.e. days). benefits using datey : consistent framework converting dates uniform annual grid. Handling often-overlooked issue whether date means start, end day. Fixed precision arithmetic, excludes bugs relating floating point arithmetic1. PUT FIRST: Long story short: working primarily annual rates annual domain data specifies time using dates, worth considering using datey. See surv package author’s comments . Note use days avoid issue. See CRAN FAQ 7.31

title: Annualised Dates and Durations
text: can install development version datey GitHub :
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("logmu-org/r-datey")

what: Coerce a calendar year (including fractional part) or another date type to a datey for the start, middle of end of the day — as_xxx_day
title: Coerce a calendar year (including fractional part) or another date type to a datey for the start, middle of end of the day — as_xxx_day
text: Accepted types : Numeric, interpreted calendar year, fractional part representing fraction year. instance, datey(2000.5) means halfway though year 2000. base R date types (Date POSIXct POSIXlt). character, ISO 8601 extended format, .e. YYYY-MM-DD. datey, interpreted start, middle end day override. Beware as_end_day() add day datey already day boundary, even originally defined end day.

title: Coerce a calendar year (including fractional part) or another date type to a datey for the start, middle of end of the day — as_xxx_day
code: as_start_day(x, strict = TRUE)  as_mid_day(x, strict = TRUE)  as_end_day(x, strict = TRUE)

title: Coerce a calendar year (including fractional part) or another date type to a datey for the start, middle of end of the day — as_xxx_day
text: x argument convert datey. strict invalid results (given non-NA inputs) handled. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NA inputs result NA regardless switch.)

what: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
title: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
text: Converts datey calendar years. fractional part represents proportion calendar year elapsed. example, start 2000-01-01 (, equivalently, end 1999-12-31), results 2000, middle calendar year 2000 results 2000.5. Note following: .numeric() .double(). .integer() gives calendar year, e.g. .integer(datey(2000.9)) 2000. .integer(x) .integer(.double(x)).

title: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
code: # S3 method for class 'datey' as.double(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' as.integer(x, ...)

title: Convert a datey to calendar years (including fractional part) — as_years_datey
text: x datey convert years. ... arguments passed methods.

what: Convert a durationy to duration in years — as_years_durationy
title: Convert a durationy to duration in years — as_years_durationy
text: Converts durationy duration years. Note following: .numeric() .double(). .integer() obtains integer part, e.g. .integer(durationy(1.9)) 1 .integer(durationy(-1.9)) -1. .integer(x) .integer(.double(x)).

Examples actuarial mortality experience analysis valuation life assurance annuities, mortality rates defined per year experience valuation data use dates.

text: Maintainer: Tim Gordon tim.gordon@btinternet.com (ORCID) Authors: Tim Gordon tim.gordon@btinternet.com (ORCID)

what: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
text: package provides methods create datey following: double integer interpreted specified calendar year, fractional part representing fraction year. instance, datey(2000.5) means halfway though year 2000. (means integer argument always indicates start calendar year, e.g. datey(2000L) start year 2000.) Date POSIXct POSIXlt interpreted fractional years. day_fraction argument provided day fraction determined hours, minutes, seconds. instance, datey(.POSIXct("2000-03-21 12:00")) means middle 2000-03-21. Note standard use, Date fractional part therefore means start day. instance, datey(.Date("2000-03-21 12:00")) means start 2000-03-21. character – day_fraction provided text format must ISO 8601 extended format, .e. YYYY-MM-DD. day_fraction provided text format must YYYY-MM-DD.FFF, .FFF day fraction must present even fraction 0, e.g. "2000-01-01.0" start 1 January 2000. datey interpreted optional day_fraction override. Note day_fraction 1 add day day boundary, even originally defined end day. lengths vector arguments must multiples . S3 generic.

title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
code: datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # Default S3 method datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'datey' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'integer' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'double' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'Date' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXct' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)  # S3 method for class 'POSIXlt' datey(x, day_fraction = NULL, strict = TRUE, ...)

title: Create a datey from a calendar year (including its fractional part) or another date type — datey
text: x argument convert datey. day_fraction day_fraction override. Defaults NULL. day_fraction provided x used derive calendar year, month, day day fraction. day_fraction provided x used solely derive calendar year, month day, day_fraction provides position day. day_fraction must lie inclusive interval [0,1], 0 meaning start day, 0.5 meaning middle day, 1 meaning end day (identical start next day). strict calendar years less 1000 greater 3000 day fractions interval [0,1] handled. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NAs result NA regardless switch.) ... arguments (used package).

what: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
title: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
text: Valid datey system ranges: Valid dates start 1000 start 3000. Valid durations 2000 years less magnitude. Values outside ranges treated NA. .na() tests whether datey durationy NA element. anyNA() tests whether element datey durationy NA. convenience, constants NA_datey_ NA_datey_ datey durationy versions NA respectively, integer constants describing valid ranges also provided. performance reasons, intermediate calculations may check NAs.

title: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
code: # S3 method for class 'datey' is.na(x)  # S3 method for class 'datey' anyNA(x, recursive = FALSE)  # S3 method for class 'durationy' is.na(x)  # S3 method for class 'durationy' anyNA(x, recursive = FALSE)

title: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
text: x datey durationy test NA recursive Currently required FALSE (default)

title: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
text: .na() returns vector logical length x. anyNA() always returns TRUE FALSE, never NA never anything single value.

title: Whether datey or durationy are NA — is_NA
code: t <- c(NA_datey_, datey(2000), datey(999.99, strict = FALSE))   is.na(t) # c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE  TRUE   anyNA(t) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE    d <- c(NA_durationy_, durationy(1.5))   is.na(d) # c(TRUE, FALSE) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE   anyNA(d) # TRUE #> [1] TRUE

title: Format or print a durationy — text_from_durationy
text: x durationy print format. include_plus Whether include plus ('+') sign positive durations. Defaults FALSE. use_true_minus Whether use true minus sign ('−', U+2212) sign opposed ASCII hyphen (-, U+2212). Defaults FALSE. year_unit year unit name print. blank value followed space unit. 20 characters (UTF-8 bytes) contain control characters. Defaults "yr". ... arguments passed methods. max Numeric NULL, specifying maximal number entries printed. NULL, getOption("max.print") used. Defaults NULL.

title: Format or print a durationy — text_from_durationy
code: pos <- durationy(1)   neg <- durationy(-2.3)   format(pos) # "1 yr" #> [1] "1 yr"   format(pos, include_plus = TRUE) # "1 yr" #> [1] "+1 yr"   format(pos, year_unit = "") # "1" #> [1] "1"   format(neg) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, use_true_minus = TRUE) # "−2.3 yr" #> [1] "−2.3 yr"   format(neg, year_unit = "a") # "-2.3 a" #> [1] "−2.3 a"

what: Create or decompose a datey using calendar year, month, day and day fraction — ymdf
title: Create or decompose a datey using calendar year, month, day and day fraction — ymdf
text: lengths vector arguments must multiples . to_ymdf() returns list year, month, day day_fraction breakdown datey, year integer [1000,3000], month integer [1,12], day integer [1,N], N number days month specified year month, day_fraction double [0,1) representing fraction day, e.g. 0 means start 0.5 means middle day. datey constructed using end_day day_fraction = 1 to_ymdf() return start next day day_fraction = 0. from_ymdf() creates datey calendar year, month, day fraction. practice, prefer one start_day(), mid_day() end_day() clarity.

title: Create or decompose a datey using calendar year, month, day and day fraction — ymdf
code: to_ymdf(datey)  from_ymdf(year, month, day, day_fraction, strict = TRUE)

title: Create or decompose a datey using calendar year, month, day and day fraction — ymdf
text: datey datey deconstructed. year Calendar year. Valid years 1000 3000 (although legal date 3000 start 3000-01-01). provided double must integers. month Month number calendar year, 1 representing January. provided double must integers. day Day number month, 1 representing first day month. provided double must integers. day_fraction fraction day, [0,1]. 0 means start day, 0.5 means middle day, 1 means end day (identical start next day). strict handle invalid arguments. strict TRUE – default – execution stopped. strict FALSE NA returned. (NA arguments result NA regardless strict.)

title: datey (development version)
text: Development version

title: datey 0.0.1
text: Initial release
